survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: SW tip of Eilean Mhic Chrion (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Mixed sediment of muddy sand with shell and stone gravel, dead shells, cobbles and some boulders, in lower infralittoral. Sabella pavonina abundant, Sagartia elegans and green weed (Chlorophycota) common.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Shelly gravel in ridges with many shells piled into depression.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: NW Eilean nan Coinean (Outer Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: 30 degree gravel slope, upper circalittoral with abundant Ophiothrix fragilis and frequent Ophiocomina nigra. From 18 -29 m BCD.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Eilean an Ruisg (Loch Feochan)
sample_comment: A plain of coarse sediment (muddy gravel) with cobbles and pebbles and a few small boulders and shells. Dense stands of Laminaria saccharina with Chorda filum and filamentous browns and foliose reds growing profusely in clearings. Sabella pavonina common amongst algae, and a few Modiolus modiolus present. Large clearings of sediment with Chone spp. and occasional Branchiomma bombyx.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E of Eilean nam Muc (Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Muddy sand with loosely attached red algae, Turritella spp. shells and scattered Modiolus modiolus clumps.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E of Eilean nam Muc (Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Bedrock with red algae and kelp.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Vertical and overhanging surfaces including a cave formed by the overhang at c. 5 to 11 m BCD. Colonised by a large variety of animals with Ascidia mentula and Sabella pavonina particularly conspicuous. Several rarely encountered species present.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Large boulders and bedrock outcrops on muddy sediments shallower than 22 m BCD. Characterised by a wide variety of animal species but with Swiftia pallida, Diazonia violacea frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Rhodophycota has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Flat muddy plain, with boulders close to the bedrock cliff. Algal cover especially foliose reds.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Bedrock outcrop from 6 - m with crevices and boulders at the bottom. Heavily grazed by Echinus esculentus.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Back Bow, Point ofv Knap (Outer Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Chondria tenuissima has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Extensive smooth bedrock dominated by encrusting brown algae with vertical and some overhanging surfaces with boulders between and fringing the muddy gravel slope. Dominated at shallow depths by dense patches of Brongniartella byssoides with Bonnemaisonia asparagoides and sparse Laminaria hyperborea and L. saccharina. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/near to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Back Bow, Point fo Knap (Outer Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Slope of muddy shell gravel with pebbles colonised by a rich fauna and flora characterised by Cerianthus lloydii with Rhizocaulus verticillatus on the pebbles. Red algae attached to pebbles.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Sgeir an t-Snidh (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Dense litter of Anomia ephippium shells on mud with live Anomia ephippium attached. Partly covered by Stictyosiphon spp. (c. 40%) with common Chorda filum. Chlamys varia frequent in clumps of Anomia ephippium.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Sgeir an t-Snidh (West Loch Tawrbert)
sample_comment: Diatoms - film has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Transition between habitats 2 and 4 but with fairly rich fauna. Chlamys varia and dead shells.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Sgeir an t-Snidh (West Loch Talrbert)
sample_comment: Mixed sediments with clumps of large Anomia ephippium providing hard substratum for attached Chorda filum. Patchs of Stictyosiphon spp. Cerianthus lloydii common in the sediment and Clavelina lepadiformis common on shells. Otherwise fairly sparse fauna and very low diversity of algae.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Bedrock with steep face covered in dense Mytilus edulis and horizontal upper surface with Laminaria saccharina and Halidrys siliquosa.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Scoul Eilean (Outer Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Rock outcrops heavily silted and covered by Ophiothrix fragliis.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: S of Eilean Traighe (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Steep-sided bedrock from -2 to -5 m, covered by fairly dense Laminaria hyperborea forest. Incompletely surveyed.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survfey
location_name: S of Eilean Traighe (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Ensis has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Foliose red algae has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value.  Plain of coarse sand, with patches of shell gravel, and a few boulders, -5 to -10 m. Large amounts of attached foliose algae. Gibbula magus and Ensis spp. common. Quite species rich.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E of Rubh an Lionaidh (Loch rCaignish)
sample_comment: Boulders and cobble bottom with occasional bedrock outcrop in moderate tidal stream with diverse red algae and sparse kelp.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E sie of Loch Caolas (Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Level muddy gravel sediment with shells dominated by Brongniatella bryssoides and characterised by Megalomma [Branchiomma] vesiculosum, Gibbula magus and terebellid spp.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E side of Loch Caolas (Loch Caoisport)
sample_comment: Broken bedrock slope with some boulders on vertical faces 80% dominated by encrusting brown algae and colonised by a sparse fauna with patches of Brongniartella bryssoides and frequent Echinus esculentus.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Upper infralittoral bedrock dropping steeply from -1 to -6 m BCD. Gentle sloping shelly mud to 15 m. Burrows and mounds common. Mya and Ensis spp. syphons common.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Pinnacle in mouth of Loch Craignish. (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Top of bedrock ridge, from -14 to -17 m, with sparse Laminaria hyperborea and understorey of foliose red algae.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Pinnacle in mouth of Loch Craignish. (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Rhodophycota has becen removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Steep bedrock with some silt cover, in upper circalittoral, -17 to -19 m. Foliose red algae common.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Boulders on bedrock at the base of Laminaria saccharina forest. Many stipes with no frond. Stipes 100% covered with Ascidiella aspersa and Ciona intestinalis. Gibbula cineria and Calliostoma zizyphinum frequent on fronds, Obelia geniculata common. On boulders of bedrock, numerous ascidians: Clavelina lepadiformis, Ascidia mentula. Polycarpa. Occasional Antedon bifida and Echinus esculentus.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealocs survey
location_name: W of Liath Sgeir Bheag (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Bedrock and boulders with a rare patch of muddy sand, in upper and lower infralittoral, -2.5 to -20.5m. Thick Laminaria hyperborea park below this. Thick understorey of foliose red algae, and rather undiverse, if rich, stipe flora. Boltenia echinata frequent on the kelp stipes. Characteristically circalittoral species such as Caryophyllia smithii, Terebratulina retusa and Diazona violacea all present. Large growths of Ascidia mentula common, as was Antedon bifida.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Shelly gravel in ridges with many shells piled into depressions.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Steep bedrock slope very exposed to tides dominated by Delesseria sanguinea and Plocamium cartilagineum with abundant Balanus crenatus. Sponges frequent, particularly Pachymatisma johnstonia and Myxilla incrustans and Botryllus leachi frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Large aind small boulders with patches of cobbles at 4 m BCD. Covered overall by very large silty fronds of Laminaria hyperborea. Rocks below largely covered by Phycodrys rubens, Delesseria sanguinea and Plocamium cartiligineum.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Bed of tidal swept channel with shell gravel and pebbles. Dense Laminaria saccharina, Saccorhiza polyschides and Chorda filum with small clearings. Many Sabella pavonina. Red algae common. Amhiura securigera frequent in the gravel.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Upper infralittoral on bedrock at 2.5 - 5.5 m BSD. Dominated by Laminaria saccharina, L. hyperborea with Membranoptera alata.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Substratum of pebbles and cobbles with some stony gravel in the interstices. A few species of encrusting bryozoans, but not many. Frequent Sabella pavonina tubes poking through the stones. Algae dominated - the main species as listed.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Pinnacle in mouth of Loch Craignish. (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Steep/vertical tide-swept lower circalittoral, -19 to -34 m. Swiftia pallida frequent. Hydroids and erect bryozoans conspicuous.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Pinnacle in mouth of Loch Craignish. (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Bryozoa has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Plain of boulders on muddy sand, sloping gradually from -34 to beyond -35 m. Pecten maximus and Ophiura albida on the sediment, Swiftia pallida frequent on the boulders, along with large numbers of bryozoans.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Liath Sgeir Bheag (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral of steep bedrock from -24.5 m followed by boulders on bedrock, a fairly level plain of boulders with muddy sand between at -41.5 m. Fauna typical of slight shelter, with frequent Swiftia pallida, Terebratulina retusa and Iophionopsis nigrescens, and occasional Axinella infundibuliformis. Neocrania anomala also frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Liath Sgeir Bheag (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Bedrock in upper circalittoral, -20.5 to -24.5. Red algae not profuse, but present, with Brongniartella byssoides and Rhodophyllis divaricata both frequent. Animal life much as in lower circalittoral (see Habitat 3).

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Large boulders and bedrock outcrops on muddy sediments shallower than 22 m BCD. Characterised by a wide variety of animal species but with Swiftia pallida, Diazonia violacea frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Shoal NE of Eilean Gamhna (Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Amphiura has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Gradual slope of muddy sediment with many boulders and cobbles. Ophiothrix fragilis dominant, Amphiura spp. in sediment. Many Marthasterias glacialis.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E side of Loch aColas (Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Plain of wave-marked coarse shell gravel, characterised by Neopentadactyla mixta and Ensis spp., with large numbers of Pomatoschistus pictus. Foliose algae attached to shells or drift in furrows. Eggs of Natica spp. present.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: NW Eilean nan Coinean (Outer Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: 30 degree gravel slope, upper circalittoral with abundant Ophiothrix fragilis and frequent Ophiocomina nigra. From 18 -29 m BCD.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyl and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E of Eilean nam Muc (Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Muddy sand with loosely attached red algae, Turritella spp. shells and scattered Modiolus modiolus clumps.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Bed of tidal swept channel with shell gravel and pebbles. Dense Laminaria saccharina, Saccorhiza polyschides and Chorda filum with small clearings. Many Sabella pavonina. Red algae common. Amhiura securigera frequent in the gravel.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Plain of muddy sand with shell debris at 8 m. Laminaria saccharina frequent. Many clumps of foliose algae particularly Polysiphonia nigrescens and Ceramium spp. Scinaia turgida frequent. Few animals although Akera bullata frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Back Bow, Point of Knap (Oguter Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Slope of muddy shell gravel with pebbles colonised by a rich fauna and flora characterised by Cerianthus lloydii with Rhizocaulus verticillatus on the pebbles. Red algae attached to pebbles.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Tide-swept plain of muddy sand with lots of pebbles aznd shells.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Plain of firm muddy sand with abundant Lanice conchilega and areas of drift algae. One area with very large numbers of Hyas araneus.

survey_name: 989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Scoul Eilean(Outer Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Infralittoral bedrock dominated by Ophiothrix fragilis with occasional Lithothamnion spp. and sparse Laminaria saccharina. Horizontal fissures with walls dominated by Pomatoceros triqueter and with Thorogobius ephippiatus and Galathea strigosa within.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Shelly mud at 7 - 3 m BCD with about 5% gravel, slope becoming more steep with an increase in depth. Characterised by Virgularia mirabilis deeper than about 6 m BCD with frequent Cerianthus lloydii, Terebellidae and Carcinus maenas.

survey_name: 1989 UMBS Msouth Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: A shallow bank of sediment, flat on top with a steep slope from ca 2-6 m BSL, with a dense bed of Lanice conchilega and several large Pagurus bernhardus wandering about.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Rather steep sediment slope with Ensis ensis regularly spaced in the sediment - quite a few Arctica islandica. Surface of the sediment looked disturbed - probably by crabs- with dead Ensis sp. shells lying around.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: NE of Eilean da Ghallagain (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Shallow (4m BCD) mud plain with many Sagartiogeton undatus, Ophiura ophiura and Pomatoschistus minitus. The sediment was covered in a diatom film and contained many burrows and holes, occupants not identified. Mud soft, and in places cobbly.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Rock including vertical and overhanging rock and fissures 4 - 7.5 m BCD with large fnumbers of Ascidia mentula and Ascidiella aspersa.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: SW tip of Eilean Mhic Chrion (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Thick Laminaria saccharina forest on bedrock and boulders, -2 to -5 m. Anemonia viridis common, diversity of species generally low.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Shelly gravel in ridges with many shells piled into depressions.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: 30 degree mud slope, 21 -23 m BCD. Burrows frequent, Swiftia pallida attached to pebbles.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Back Boxw, Point of Knap (Outer Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Chondria tenuissima has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Extensive smooth bedrock dominated by encrusting brown algae with vertical and some overhanging surfaces with boulders between and fringing the muddy gravel slope. Dominated at shallow depths by dense patches of Brongniartella byssoides with Bonnemaisonia asparagoides and sparse Laminaria hyperborea and L. saccharina. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/near to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Sgeir an t-Snidh (West Loch Tarbetr)
sample_comment: Dense litter of Anomia ephippium shells on mud with live Anomia ephippium attached. Partly covered by Stictyosiphon spp. (c. 40%) with common Chorda filum. Chlamys varia frequent in clumps of Anomia ephippium.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N o Sgeir an t-Snidh (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Diatoms - film has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Transition between habitats 2 and 4 but with fairly rich fauna. Chlamys varia and dead shells.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Gently sloping soft muddy site with abundant Ophiura ophiura and Philine aperta. Occasional Nephrops norvegicus burrows and Trachythyone elongata.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: S of Eilean Traighe (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Ensis has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Foliose red algae has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value.  Plain of coarse sand, with patches of shell gravel, and a few boulders, -5 to -10 m. Large amounts of attached foliose algae. Gibbula magus and Ensis spp. common. Quite species rich.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Steep slope of stable clay mud with many dead Turritella communis. A lot of Nephrops norvegicus burrows.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: SW of Eilean na Bruachain (Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Plain of mud at 15 m bcd with many empty Turritella communis shells. Abundant Amphiura filiformis with occasional small Virgularia mirabilis and occasional Ceriathus lloydii.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Channel floor of cobbles, boulder and pebbles, highly tide scoured. Dominated by hydroids and bryozoans, particularly Sertularia argentea, Hydrallmania falcata, Eucratea loricata and Vesicularia spinosa.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral plain of muddy sand with cobbles at -8 to -9 m. Sand cover - tolerant algae, including Scinaia turgida, Gracilaria verrucosa and Audouinella spp. present. Gibbula magus occasional. Chorda filum common and Asperococcus turneri frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Back Bow, Poitn of Knap (Outer Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Chondria tenuissima has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Extensive smooth bedrock dominated by encrusting brown algae with vertical and some overhanging surfaces with boulders between and fringing the muddy gravel slope. Dominated at shallow depths by dense patches of Brongniartella byssoides with Bonnemaisonia asparagoides and sparse Laminaria hyperborea and L. saccharina. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/near to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Substratum of pebbles and cobbles with some stony gravel in the interstices. A few species of encrusting bryozoans, but not many. Frequent Sabella pavonina tubes poking through the stones. Algae dominated - the main species as listed.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Muddy slope with shell debris and cobbles with much filamentous algae. Diatom mat on the sediment surface. Algae petered out at 16 m and Pennatula phosphorea became more numerous.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Shoal NE of Eilean Gamhna (Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Steep slope of muddy sediment with many boulders and cobbles of various sizes. Protanthea simplex and Neocrania anomala on larger boulders, Antedon petasus on cobbles. Some Amphiura chiajei and A. filiformis in the sediment. Nemertesia ramosa frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Shoal NE of Eilean Gamhna (Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Amphiura has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Gradual slope of muddy sediment with many boulders and cobbles. Ophiothrix fragilis dominant, Amphiura spp. in sediment. Many Marthasterias glacialis.

survey_name: 1998 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Stepped steeply-sloping bedrock at 20 to 22 m BCD dominated by encrusting Corallinaceae (90%) and encrusting dark red algae (10%) covered overall by Ophiothrix fragilis with fairly irch variety of species.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E of Rubh an wLionaidh (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Boulders and cobble bottom with occasional bedrock outcrop in moderate tidal stream with diverse red algae and sparse kelp.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Amphiura has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Mud slope with some very small rock outcrops. Amphiura spp. abundant. Many Chaetopterus variopedatsu on the sediment surface with Protanthea simplex attached.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Shoal SW of Liath-sgeir MhÂ¢r (Outer Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Upward surfaces of current swept bedrock ridge, with a few boulders. The bedrock was dominated by Nemertesia antennina.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Shoal SW of Liath-sgeir MhÂ¢r (Outer Loh Craignish)
sample_comment: Steep, lower circalittoral bedrock slope, current swept and dominated by Abietinaria abietina. The bedrock was smooth and with a few deep crevices. The encrusting fauna was pretty but not particularly diverse.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Pinnacle in mouth of Loch Craignish. (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Steep/vertical tide-swept lower circalittoral, -19 to -34 m. Swiftia pallida frequent. Hydroids and erect bryozoans conspicuous.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Pinnacle in mouth of Loch Craignish. (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Top of bedrock ridge, from -14 to -17 m, with sparse Laminaria hyperborea and understorey of foliose red algae.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Aryll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Boulders on bedrock at the base of Laminaria saccharina forest. Many stipes with no frond. Stipes 100% covered with Ascidiella aspersa and Ciona intestinalis. Gibbula cineria and Calliostoma zizyphinum frequent on fronds, Obelia geniculata common. On boulders of bedrock, numerous ascidians: Clavelina lepadiformis, Ascidia mentula. Polycarpa. Occasional Antedon bifida and Echinus esculentus.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Rhodophycota indet.(non-calc.crusts) has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. A vertical bedrock wall heavily urchin grazed. A number of fissures and small crevices with Neocrania anomala. Ciona intestinalis, Corella parallelogramma common. Rock heavily silted.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: A steep sediment slope with Virgularia mirabilis, Corymorpha nutans and Amphiura spp. Virgularia mirabilis not present below 27m, the mud content increased with depth. Molgula occulta in sediment at top of the slope.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Liath Sgeir Bheag (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Bedrock in upper circalittoral, -20.5 to -24.5. Red algae not profuse, but present, with Brongniartella byssoides and Rhodophyllis divaricata both frequent. Animal life much as in lower circalittoral (see Habitat 3).

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Liath Sgeir Bheag (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Bedrock and boulders with a rare patch of muddy sand, in upper and lower infralittoral, -2.5 to -20.5m. Thick Laminaria hyperborea park below this. Thick understorey of foliose red algae, and rather undiverse, if rich, stipe flora. Boltenia echinata frequent on the kelp stipes. Characteristically circalittoral species such as Caryophyllia smithii, Terebratulina retusa and Diazona violacea all present. Large growths of Ascidia mentula common, as was Antedon bifida.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Shelly gravel in ridges with many shells piled into depressions.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Steep bedrock slope very exposed to tides dominated by Deleseria sanguinea and Plocamium cartilagineum with abundant Balanus crenatus. Sponges frequent, particularly Pachymatisma johnstonia and Myxilla incrustans and Botryllus leachi frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Channel floor of cobbles, boulder and pebbles, hihgly tide scoured. Dominated by hydroids and bryozoans, particularly Sertularia argentea, Hydrallmania falcata, Eucratea loricata and Vesicularia spinosa.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintiyre sealochs survey
location_name: E of Eilean nam Muc (Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Muddy sand with loosely attached red algae, Turritella spp. shells and scattered Modiolus modiolus clumps.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs srvey
sample_comment: Large boulders and bedrock outcrops on muddy sediments shallower than 22 m BCD. Characterised by a wide variety of animal species but with Swiftia pallida, Diazonia violacea frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Back Bow, Point fo Knap (Outer Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Chondria tenuissima has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Extensive smooth bedrock dominated by encrusting brown algae with vertical and some overhanging surfaces with boulders between and fringing the muddy gravel slope. Dominated at shallow depths by dense patches of Brongniartella byssoides with Bonnemaisonia asparagoides and sparse Laminaria hyperborea and L. saccharina. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/near to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Scoul Eilean (Outer Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Rock outcrops heavily silted and covered by Ophiothrix fragilis.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Bed of tidal swept channel with shell gravel and pebbles. Dense Laminaria saccharina, Saccorhiza polyschides and Chorda filum with small clearings. Many Sabella pavonina. Red algae common. Amhiura securigera frequent in the gravel.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Upper infralittoral bedrock with some boulders. Forest of Laminaria hyperborea with dense algal undergrowth, including Plocamium cartilagineum, Cryptopleura ramosa, Callophyllis lacinata, Delesseria sanguinea, Cystoclonium purpureum, Rhodomela confervoides etc.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Shelly gravel in ridges with many shells piled into depressions.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Plain of muddy shell gravel with cobbles and pebbles. Much algal debris and clumps of living algae attached to the pebbles. Phyllophora crispa and Gymnogongrus crenatus common.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Bed of tidal swept channel with shell gravel and pebbles. Dense Laminaria saccharina, Saccorhiza polyschides and Chorda filum with small clearings. Many Sabella pavonina. Red algae common. Amhiura securigera frequent in the gravel.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Boulder tumble at 9 - 10 m dominated (60%) by encrusting Corallinacea and (40%) encrusting dark red Rhodophycota with common Tectura virginea. Abundant Laminaria saccharina overall with scattered foliose red algae in patches.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Eilean an Ruisg (Loch Feochan)
sample_comment: A plain of coarse sediment (muddy gravel) with cobbles and pebbles and a few small boulders and shells. Dense stands of Laminaria saccharina with Chorda filum and filamentous browns and foliose reds growing profusely in clearings. Sabella pavonina common amongst algae, and a few Modiolus modiolus present. Large clearings of sediment with Chone spp. and occasional Branchiomma bombyx.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Rhodophycota has been removed from the species list for this record as more specfic related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Flat muddy plain, with boulders close to the bedrock cliff. Algal cover especially foliose reds.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Substratum of pebbles and cobbles with some stony gravel in the interstices. A few species of encrusting bryozoans, but not many. Frequent Sabella pavonina tubes poking through the stones. Algae dominated - the main species as listed.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Back Bow, Point of Knap (Oter Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Slope of muddy shell gravel with pebbles colonised by a rich fauna and flora characterised by Cerianthus lloydii with Rhizocaulus verticillatus on the pebbles. Red algae attached to pebbles.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Boulder tumble at 9 - 10 m dominated (60%) by encrusting Corallinacea and (40%) encrusting dark red Rhodpohycota with common Tectura virginea. Abundant Laminaria saccharina overall with scattered foliose red algae in patches.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Large and small boulders with patches of cobbles at 4 m BCD. Covered overall by very large silty fronds of Laminaria hyperborea. Rocks below largely covered by Phycodrys rubens, Delesseria sanguinea and Plocamium cartiligineum.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Upper infralittoral on bedrock at 2.5 5.5 m BSD. Dominated by Laminaria saccharina, L. hyperborea with Membranoptera alata.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyllg and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Back Bow, Point of Knap (Outer Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Chondria tenuissima has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Extensive smooth bedrock dominated by encrusting brown algae with vertical and some overhanging surfaces with boulders between and fringing the muddy gravel slope. Dominated at shallow depths by dense patches of Brongniartella byssoides with Bonnemaisonia asparagoides and sparse Laminaria hyperborea and L. saccharina. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/near to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Muddy shell gravel plain with very occasiona cobbles at -3.8 to -5.8 m. No dominant species, just a mix of algae on cobbles and shallow sand species.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral and upper circalittoral cobbles, pebbles and shell gravel from 9 m to 15 m. Delessera sanguinea and Phycodrys rubens , Phyllophora crispa and Dilsea carnosa common, with various other red algae, Enteromorpha spp. and Ulva spp., Pomatoceros triqueter, Pododesmus patelliformis, Galathea intermedia, prawns and shrimps and two-spotted gobies and terebellids. Dendrodoa grossularia common. Laminaria saccharina to 12 m BSL. The seabed was a dense carpet of foliose algae with drift green algae intermixed and only a few small bare patches of sediment with Amphitrite spp.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Upper infralittoral with 1 m cliff down to 2.5 m BCD. Phoronis hippocrepia at base of the cliff with Cereus pedunculatus.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochss urvey
sample_comment: Shelly gravel in ridges with many shells piled into depressions.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: S of Eilean Traighe (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Steep-sided bedrock from -2 to -5 m, covered by fairly dense Laminaria hyperborea forest. Incompletely surveyed.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: S of Eilean Traighe (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Ensis has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Foliose red algae has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value.  Plain of coarse sand, with patches of shell gravel, and a few boulders, -5 to -10 m. Large amounts of attached foliose algae. Gibbula magus and Ensis spp. common. Quite species rich.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Channel floor of cobbles, boulder and pebbles, highly tide scoured. Dominated by hydroids and bryozoans, particularly Sertularia argentea, Hydrallmania falcata, Eucratea loricata and Vesicularia spinosa.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Soft muddy sediment with approximately 20% shelly gravel at 3 - 2 m BCD. Mainly covered by algae especially Stictyosiphon spp. with Laminaria saccharina. Sabella pavonina frequent to common.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Flat soft sediment at 13 m BCD. Covered (70%) by filamentous green algae but with common Pomataschistus minutus and frequent P. pictus and Arenicola marina in the clearings. Some areas of Beggiatoa spp.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Sgeir an t-Snidh (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Dense litter of Anomia ephippium shells on mud with live Anomia ephippium attached. Partly covered by Stictyosiphon spp. (c. 40%) with common Chorda filum. Chlamys varia frequent in clumps of Anomia ephippium.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: bN of Sgeir an t-Snidh (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Diatoms - film has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Transition between habitats 2 and 4 but with fairly rich fauna. Chlamys varia and dead shells.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Sgeir an t-Snidh (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Mixed sediments with clumps of large Anomia ephippium providing hard substratum for attached Chorda filum. Patchs of Stictyosiphon spp. Cerianthus lloydii common in the sediment and Clavelina lepadiformis common on shells. Otherwise fairly sparse fauna and very low diversity of algae.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: S of Eilean Traighe (est Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Ensis has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Foliose red algae has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value.  Plain of coarse sand, with patches of shell gravel, and a few boulders, -5 to -10 m. Large amounts of attached foliose algae. Gibbula magus and Ensis spp. common. Quite species rich.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Scoul Eilean (Outer Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Mud with many burrows and dense Amphiura filiformis and A. chiajei. Layer of diatoms on the surface of the mud.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Rather steep sediment slope with Ensis ensis regularly spaced in the sediment - quite a few Arctica islandica. Surface of the sediment looked disturbed - probably by crabs- with dead Ensis sp. shells lying around.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Shelly gravel, lower infralittoral with burrows, especially Mya truncata and Enssi ensis, Turritella communis frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Shoal NE of Eilean Gamhna (Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Steep slope of muddy sediment with many boulders and cobbles of various sizes. Protanthea simplex and Neocrania anomala on larger boulders, Antedon petasus on cobbles. Some Amphiura chiajei and A. filiformis in the sediment. Nemertesia ramosa frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM osuth Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Shoal NE of Eilean Gamhna (Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Amphiura has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Gradual slope of muddy sediment with many boulders and cobbles. Ophiothrix fragilis dominant, Amphiura spp. in sediment. Many Marthasterias glacialis.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochss survey
location_name: Shoal NE of Eilean Gamhna (Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Steep slope of smooth silty bedrock from 19 to 33 m BCD with many Antedon petasus, some Ophiura albida, Marthasterias glacialis, Astropecten irregularis. Rather barren.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Amphiura has been removed from the species list for this record as more specifc related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Mud slope with some very small rock outcrops. Amphiura spp. abundant. Many Chaetopterus variopedatus on the sediment surface with Protanthea simplex attached.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Slope of silty bedrock with very ridged surface. Encrusting coralline algae abundant. Protanthea simplex abundant particularly in deeper rock. Chaetopterus variopedatus frequent. Many Marthasterias glacialis.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Rubh' an Tighe Loisgte (Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Steep soft sediment slope (45 degrees) with frequent Liocarcinus depurator.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: NE of Campbell Rock( Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Steeply sloping bedrock from -16 to -24 m BCD, well silted with abundant Ophiothrix fragilis, occasional Ophiocomina nigra, Protanthea simplex and encrusting coralline algae. Rocks below Ophiothrix fragilis were generally dominated by encrusting Corallinaceae at least in shallower depths and with dense Chaetopterus variopedatus attached to rock.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Sloping mud with abundant Ophiothrix fragilis, occasional Chaetopterus variopedatus tubes lying on the surface of the mud.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: SW of Rubh Ard an Stirra (Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Bedrock wth Ophiothrix fragilis blanket and cover of coralline crusts.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: SW of Rubh Ard an Stirra (Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Shallow mud slope with Amphiura spp. and many loose Chaetopterus variopedatus tubes. Patches of Ophiothrix fragilis.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Scoul Eilean (Outer Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Rock outcrops heavily silted and covered by Ophiothrix fragilis.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: S of Eilean Traighe (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Ensis has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Foliose red algae has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value.  Plain of coarse sand, with patches of shell gravel, and a few boulders, -5 to -10 m. Large amounts of attached foliose algae. Gibbula magus and Ensis spp. common. Quite species rich.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: SW tip of Eilean Mhic Chrion (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Mixed sediment of muddy sand with shell and stone gravel, dead shells, cobbles and some boulders, in lower infralittoral. Sabella pavonina abundant, Sagartia elegans and green weed (Chlorophycota) common.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Small cliff at -15 to -19 m with much silt on the rock surfaces. Rock heavily encrusted with barnacles and sponges and ascidians. Polymastia mamillaris and Suberites carnosus frequent. Many Epizoanthus couchii and Edwardsiella carnea. Mats of tiny sabellid worms ( cf Abereiddy Quarry - ask Dale Rostron). Nemertesia ramosa frequent along the top edge of the cliff.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Substratum of pebbles and cobbles with some stony gravel in the interstices. A few species of encrusting bryozoans, but not many. Frequent Sabella pavonina tubes poking through the stones. Algae dominated - the main species as listed.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Scoul Eilean (Outer Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Rock outcrops heavily silted and covered by Ophiothrix fragilis.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Rather steep sediment slope with Ensis ensis regularly spaced in the sediment - quite a few Arctica islandica. Surface of the sediment looked disturbed - probably by crabs- with dead Ensis sp. shells lying around.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Near horizontal plain of very soft mud with small quantity of shell fragments. Surface slightly uneven with rare burrows. Extensive clumps of Sabella pavonina with abundant Ascidiella aspersa and Antedon bifida on the Sabella pavonina. Mud surface festooned with small worm tubes (3-4mm) above surface with isolated individuals of; Thyone fusus, Amphitrite sp., Myxicola infundibulum, Mya truncata, Virgularia mirabilis, Sabella pavonina, clumps of Ascidiella aspersa with frequent Amphilectus fucorum and Halichondria bowerbanki, which in turn supported Leucosolinia sp.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Plain of mud 19 m BCD with small Virgularia mirabilis, occasional Cerianthus lloydii, Turritella communis, Lanice conchilega and rarely Arctica islandica.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Shoal SW of Liath-sgeir MhÂ¢r (Outer Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Upward surfaces of current swept bedrock ridge, with a few boulders. The bedrock was dominated by Nemertesia antennina.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Liath Sgeir Bheag (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Bedrock and boulders with a rare patch of muddy sand, in upper and lower infralittoral, -2.5 to -20.5m. Thick Laminaria hyperborea park below this. Thick understorey of foliose red algae, and rather undiverse, if rich, stipe flora. Boltenia echinata frequent on the kelp stipes. Characteristically circalittoral species such as Caryophyllia smithii, Terebratulina retusa and Diazona violacea all present. Large growths of Ascidia mentula common, as was Antedon bifida.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Channel floor of cobbles, boulder and pebbles, highly tide scoured. Dominated by hydroids and bryozoans, particularly Sertularia argentea, Hydrallmania falcata, Eucratea loricata and Vesicularia spinosa.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Stepped steeply-sloping bedrock at 20 to 22 m BCD dominated by encrusting Corallinaceae (90%) and encrusting dark red algae (10%) covered overall by Ophiothrixu fragilis with fairly rich variety of species.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Rhodophycota indet.(non-calc.crusts), Plagiospora has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Tumble of boulders with small patches of shell and shell gravel at 27 to 29 m BCD dominated by encrusting Corallinaceae (60%) and encrusting dark red algae (30%) with Ophiothrix fragilis overall. (Very similar to comments on Habitat 1 ).

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Shoal SW of Liath-sgeir MhÂ¢r n(Outer Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Upward surfaces of current swept bedrock ridge, with a few boulders. The bedrock was dominated by Nemertesia antennina.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E side of Lochx Caolas (Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Smooth rounded bedrock dominated by kelp and Desmarestia aculeata at 6 to 10 m BCD.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Shelly gravel, lower infralittoral wih burrows, especially Mya truncata and Ensis ensis, Turritella communis frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Flat bedrock at 2m BCD with Corallinacea crusts. Dominated by Laminaria saccharina with some Laminaria digitata overall with Plocamium cartilagineum dominant below. 2-spot gobies frequent over the algae.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Pinnacle in mouth of Loch Craignish. (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Steep/vertical tide-swept lower circalittoral, -19 to -34 m. Swiftia pallida frequent. Hydroids and erect bryozoans conspicuous.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Pinnacle in mouth of Loch Craignish. (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Top of bedrock ridge, from -14 to -17 m, with sparse Laminaria hyperborea and understorey of foliose red algae.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Pinnacle in mouth of Loch Craignish. (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Rhodophycota has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Steep bedrock with some silt cover, in upper circalittoral, -17 to -19 m. Foliose red algae common.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Boulders on bedrock at the base of Laminaria saccharina forest. Many stipes with no frond. Stipes 100% covered with Ascidiella aspersa and Ciona intestinalis. Gibbula cineria and Calliostoma zizyphinum frequent on fronds, Obelia geniculata common. On boulders of bedrock, numerous ascidians: Clavelina lepadiformis, Ascidia mentula. Polycarpa. Occasional Antedon bifida and Echinus esculentus.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Liath Sgeir Bheag (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral of steep bedrock from -24.5 m followed by boulders on bedrock, a fairly level plain of boulders with muddy sand between at -41.5 m. Fauna typical of slight shelter, with frequent Swiftia pallida, Terebratulina retusa and Iophionopsis nigrescens, and occasional Axinella infundibuliformis. Neocrania anomala also frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Liath Sgeir Bheag (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Bedrock in upper circalittoral, -20.5 to -24.5. Red algae not profuse, but present, with Brongniartella byssoides and Rhodophyllis divaricata both frequent. Animal life much as in lower circalittoral (see Habitat 3).

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Liath Sgeir Bheag (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Bedrock and boulders with a rare patch of muddy sand, in upper and lower infralittoral, -2.5 to -20.5m. Thick Laminaria hyperborea park below this. Thick understorey of foliose red algae, and rather undiverse, if rich, stipe flora. Boltenia echinata frequent on the kelp stipes. Characteristically circalittoral species such as Caryophyllia smithii, Terebratulina retusa and Diazona violacea all present. Large growths of Ascidia mentula common, as was Antedon bifida.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Upper infralittoral bedrock with some boulders. Forest of Laminaria hyperborea with dense algal undergrowth, including Plocamium cartilagineum, Cryptopleura ramosa, Callophyllis lacinata, Delesseria sanguinea, Cystoclonium purpureum, Rhodomela confervoidese tc.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: SW tip of Eilean Mhic Chrion (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Thick Laminaria saccharina forest on bedrock and boulders, -2 to -5 m. Anemonia viridis common, diversity of species generally low.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Scoul Eileaxn (Outer Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Muddy fine sand with some shell gravel and medium sand. Subsurface coarser layer of empty shells (possibly Mya spp). Dominant specise Amphiura chiajei with some clumps of algae.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll an west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Silted rock largely bare below the silt with sparse attachd flora and fauna including occasional spots of encrusting Corallinacea and colonised by scattered worm/amphipod tubes. Dendrodoa grossularia, Lepidochitona cinerea, Polymastia mamillaris and filamentous red algae etc.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Ea of Rubh an Lionaidh (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Boulders and cobble bottom with occasional bedrock outcrop in moderate tidal stream with diverse red algae and sparse kelp.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Slope of silty bedrock with very ridged surface. Encrusting coralline algae abundant. Protanthea simplex abundant particularly in deeper rock. Chaetopterus variopedatus frequent. Many Marthasterias glacialis.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Shoal SW of Liath-sgeir MhÂ¢r (Outer Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Upward surfaces of current swept bedrock ridge, with a few boulders. The bedrock was dominated by Nemertesia antennina.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Steep mud slope with numerous cobbles and pebbles. Modiolus modiolus frequent from 9m upwards. Kirchenpaueria pinnata frequent attached to the cobbles.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Small cliff at -15 to -19 m with much silt on the rock surfaces. Rock heavily encrusted with barnacls and sponges and ascidians. Polymastia mamillaris and Suberites carnosus frequent. Many Epizoanthus couchii and Edwardsiella carnea. Mats of tiny sabellid worms ( cf Abereiddy Quarry - ask Dale Rostron). Nemertesia ramosa frequent along the top edge of the cliff.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Rock including vertical and overhanging rock and fissures 4 - 7.5 m BCD with large numbers of Ascidia mentula and Ascidiella aspersa.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Pinnacle in mouth of Loch Craignish. (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Top of bedrock ridge, from -14 to -17 m, with sparse Laminaria hyperborea and understorey of foliose red algae.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Shelly gravel in ridges with many shells piled into depressions.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Plain of muddy shell gravel with cobbles and pebbles. Much algal debris and clumps of living algae attached to the pebbles. Phyllophora crispa and Gymnogongrus crenatus common.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Bed of tidal swept channel with shell gravel and pebbles. Dense Laminaria saccharina, Saccorhiza polyschides and Chorda filum with small clearings. Many Sabella pavonina. Red algae common. Amhiura securigera frequent in the gravel.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Steep bedrock slope very exposed to tides dominated by Delesseria sanguinea and Plocamium cartilagineum with abundant Balanus crenatus. Sponges frequent, particularly Pachymatisma johnstonia and Myxilla incrustans and Botryllus leachi frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Channel floor of cobbles, boulder and pebbles, highly tide scoured. Dominated by hydroids and bryozoans, particularly Sertularia argentea, Hydrallmania falcata, Eucratea loricata and Vesicularia spinosa.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Scoul Eilean (Outer Loch bMelfort)
sample_comment: Infralittoral bedrock dominated by Ophiothrix fragilis with occasional Lithothamnion spp. and sparse Laminaria saccharina. Horizontal fissures with walls dominated by Pomatoceros triqueter and with Thorogobius ephippiatus and Galathea strigosa within.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealogchs survey
sample_comment: Shelly mud at 7 - 3 m BCD with about 5% gravel, slope becoming more steep with an increase in depth. Characterised by Virgularia mirabilis deeper than about 6 m BCD with frequent Cerianthus lloydii, Terebellidae and Carcinus maenas.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM sout hArgyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: A shallow bank of sediment, flat on top with a steep slope from ca 2-6 m BSL, with a dense bed of Lanice conchilega and several large Pagurus bernhardus wandering about.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Rather steep sediment slope with Ensis ensis regularly spaced in the sediment - quite a few Arctica islandica. Surface of the sediment looked disturbed - probably by crabs- with dead Ensis sp. shells lying around.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south rgyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral and upper circalittoral cobbles, pebbles and shell gravel from 9 m to 15 m. Delessera sanguinea and Phycodrys rubens , Phyllophora crispa and Dilsea carnosa common, with various other red algae, Enteromorpha spp. and Ulva spp., Pomatoceros triqueter, Pododesmus patelliformis, Galathea intermedia, prawns and shrimps and two-spotted gobies and terebellids. Dendrodoa grossularia common. Laminaria saccharina to 12 m BSL. The seabed was a dense carpet of foliose algae with drift green algae intermixed and only a few small bare patches of sediment with Amphitrite spp.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Rubh' an Tighe Loisgte (Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Steep slope of bedrock and boulders and cobbles on shelly mud. Rather impoverished.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Rubh' an Tighe Loisgte (Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Steep, algal covered infralittoral bedrock dominated by Desmarestia aculeata, Asperococcus spp. and Laminaria saccharina, from 0 to 6.5 m BCD.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Holothurioidea has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Soft grey mud with shell gravel and whole dead shell. Surface dominated by small mud polychaete tubes (2-3cms), large clumps of ascidians and single large specimens, Modiolus (frequent), Sabella (frequent) and holothurians. Mud excavated more extensively above 18m.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Shelly gravel in ridges with many shells piled into depressions.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: NW Eilean nan Coinean (Outer Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: 30 degree gravel slope, upper circalittoral with abundant Ophiothrix fragilis and frequent Ophiocomina nigra. From 18 -29 m BCD.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Channel floor of cobbles, boulder and pebbles, highly tide scoured. Dominated by hydroids and bryozoans, particularly Sertularia argentea, Hydrallmania falcata, Eucratea loricata and Vesicularia spinosa.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E of Sger Mheim (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: A bare plain of mud with some hydroids attached to shells, burrows with Nephrops norvegicus, Virgularia mirabilis and Sagartiogeton undatus occasional. At the time of the dive there were large numbers of broken Virgularia mirabilis at the seabed.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: 30 degree mud slope, 21 -23 m BCD. Burrows frequent, Swiftia pallida attached to pebbles.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Back Bow, Point of Knap (uter Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Chondria tenuissima has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Extensive smooth bedrock dominated by encrusting brown algae with vertical and some overhanging surfaces with boulders between and fringing the muddy gravel slope. Dominated at shallow depths by dense patches of Brongniartella byssoides with Bonnemaisonia asparagoides and sparse Laminaria hyperborea and L. saccharina. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/near to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Arkgyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Upper infralittoral bedrock with some boulders. Forest of Laminaria hyperborea with dense algal undergrowth, including Plocamium cartilagineum, Cryptopleura ramosa, Callophyllis lacinata, Delesseria sanguinea, Cystoclonium purpureum, Rhodomela confervoides etc.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Plain of muddy shell gravel with cobbles and pebbles. Much algal debris and clumps of living algae attached to the pebbles. Phyllophora crispa and Gymnogongrus crenatus common.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Bed of tidal swept channel with shell gravel and pebbles. Dense Laminaria saccharina, Saccorhiza polyschides and Chorda filum with small clearings. Many Sabella pavonina. Red algae common. Amhiura securigera frequent in the gravel.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Steep bedrock slope very exposed to tides dominated by Delesseria sanguinea and Plocamium cartilagineum with abundant Balanus crenatus. Sponges frequent, particularly Pachymatisma johnstonia and Myxilla incrustans and Botryllus leachi frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Boulder tumble at 9 - 10 m dominated (60%) by encrusting Corallinacea and (40%) encrusting dark red Rhodophycota with common Tectura virginea.A bundant Laminaria saccharina overall with scattered foliose red algae in patches.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Large and small boulders with patches of cobbles at 4 m BCD. Covered overall by very large silty fronds of Laminaria hyperborea. Rocks below largely covered by Phycodrys rubens, Delesseria sanguinea and Plocamium cartiligineum.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Eilean an Ruisg (Loch Feochan)
sample_comment: A plain of coarse sediment (muddy gravel) with cobbles and pebbles and a few small boulders and shells. Dense stands of Laminaria saccharina with Chorda filum and filamentous browns and foliose reds growing profusely in clearings. Sabella pavonina common amongst algae, and a few Modiolus modiolus present. Large clearings of sediment with Chone spp. and occasional Branchiomma bombyx.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Scoul Eilean (Outer Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Mud with many burrows and dense Amphiura filiformis and A. chiajei. Layer of diatoms on the surface of the mud.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kinotyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Vertical and overhanging surfaces including a cave formed by the overhang at c. 5 to 11 m BCD. Colonised by a large variety of animals with Ascidia mentula and Sabella pavonina particularly conspicuous. Several rarely encountered species present.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: S of Eilean Traighe (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Steep-sided bedrock from -2 to -5 m, covered by fairly dense Laminaria hyperborea forest. Incompletely surveyed.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Liath Sgeir Bheag (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Bedrock and boulders with a rare patch of muddy sand, in upper and lower infralittoral, -2.5 to -20.5m. Thick Laminaria hyperborea park below this. Thick understorey of foliose red algae, and rather undiverse, if rich, stipe flora. Boltenia echinata frequent on the kelp stipes. Characteristically circalittoral species such as Caryophyllia smithii, Terebratulina retusa and Diazona violacea all present. Large growths of Ascidia mentula common, as was Antedon bifida.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Shelly gravel in ridges with many shells piled into deprsesions.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Rhodophycota indet.(non-calc.crusts), Plagiospora has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Tumble of boulders with small patches of shell and shell gravel at 27 to 29 m BCD dominated by encrusting Corallinaceae (60%) and encrusting dark red algae (30%) with Ophiothrix fragilis overall. (Very similar to comments on Habitat 1 ).

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Lower infralittoral and upper circalittoral cobbles, pebbles and shell gravel from 9 m to 15 m. Delessera sanguinea and Phycodrys rubens , Phyllophora crispa and Dilsea carnosa common, with various other red algae, Enteromorpha spp. and Ulva spp., Pomatoceros triqueter, Pododesmus patelliformis, Galathea intermedia, prawns and shrimps and two-spotted gobies and terebellids. Dendrodoa grossularia common. Laminaria saccharina to 12 m BSL. The seabed was a dense carpet of foliose algae with drift green algae intermixed and only a few small bare patches of sediment with Amphitrite spp.

survey_name: 189 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: W of Scoul Eilean (Outer Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Rock outcrops heavily silted and covered by Ophiothrix fragilis.

sample_comment: Bed of tidal swept channel with shell gravel and pebbles. Dense Laminaria saccharina, Saccorhiza polyschides and Chorda filum with small clearings. Many Sabella pavonina. Red algae common. Amhiura securigera frequent in the gravel.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E side of Loch Caolas (och Caolisport)
sample_comment: Plain of wave-marked coarse shell gravel, characterised by Neopentadactyla mixta and Ensis spp., with large numbers of Pomatoschistus pictus. Foliose algae attached to shells or drift in furrows. Eggs of Natica spp. present.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E side ofL och Caolas (Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Level muddy gravel sediment with shells dominated by Brongniatella bryssoides and characterised by Megalomma [Branchiomma] vesiculosum, Gibbula magus and terebellid spp.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Stotfiedl Bay (Outer Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Flat sand with shells and Arenicola marina mounds. Chorda filum and red algae on shells.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Shelly gravel in ridges with many shells piled into depressions.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Bed of tidal swept channel with shell gravel and pebbles. Dense Laminaria saccharina, Saccorhiza polyschides and Chorda filum with small clearings. Many Sabella pavonina. Red algae common. Amhiura securigera frequent in the gravel.

sample_comment: 30 degree gravel slope, upper circalittoral with abundant Ophiothrix fragilis and frequent Ophiocomina nigra. From 18 -29 m BCD.

sample_comment: Bed of tidal swept channel with shell gravel and pebbles. Dense Laminaria saccharina, Saccorhiza polyschides and Chorda filum with small clearings. Many Sabella pavonina. Red algae common. Amhiura securigera frequent in the gravel.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Shore of SW Liath Eilean (Loch Caolispot)
sample_comment: Steep bedrock sublittoral fringe dominated by Laminaria digitata.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Shore of SW Liath Eilean (Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Steep bedrock sublittoral fringe dominated by Laminaria digitata.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Upper infralittoral on bedrock at 2.5 - 5.5 m BSD. Dominated by Laminaria saccharina, L. hyperborea with Membranoptera alatwa.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Bedrock outcrop from 6 - 9 m with crevices and boulders at the bottom. Heavily grazed by Echinus esculentus.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Plain of muddy sand with shell debris at 8 m. Laminaria saccharina frequent. Many clumps of foliose algae particularly Polysiphonia nigrescens and Ceramium spp. Scinaia turgida frequent. Few animals although Akera bullata frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Back Bow, Point of Knap (Outer Loch Csaolisport)
sample_comment: Chondria tenuissima has been given a nominal abundance value of Present for this record as in Arev it had no abundance value. Extensive smooth bedrock dominated by encrusting brown algae with vertical and some overhanging surfaces with boulders between and fringing the muddy gravel slope. Dominated at shallow depths by dense patches of Brongniartella byssoides with Bonnemaisonia asparagoides and sparse Laminaria hyperborea and L. saccharina. Deeper rocks grazed by Echinus esculentus but with Parasmittina trispinosa and Alcyonium digitatum characterising vertical or overhanging surfaces. Boulders on/near to the sediment dominated by Balanus crenatus and Rhizocaulus verticillatus in places.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Back Bow, Point ofK nap (Outer Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Slope of muddy shell gravel with pebbles colonised by a rich fauna and flora characterised by Cerianthus lloydii with Rhizocaulus verticillatus on the pebbles. Red algae attached to pebbles.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Sgeirc an t-Snidh (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Dense litter of Anomia ephippium shells on mud with live Anomia ephippium attached. Partly covered by Stictyosiphon spp. (c. 40%) with common Chorda filum. Chlamys varia frequent in clumps of Anomia ephippium.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: S of Eilean Tarighe (West Loch Tarbert)
sample_comment: Steep-sided bedrock from -2 to -5 m, covered by fairly dense Laminaria hyperborea forest. Incompletely surveyed.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealoch ssurvey
location_name: N of Eilean an Ruisg (Loch Feochan)
sample_comment: A plain of coarse sediment (muddy gravel) with cobbles and pebbles and a few small boulders and shells. Dense stands of Laminaria saccharina with Chorda filum and filamentous browns and foliose reds growing profusely in clearings. Sabella pavonina common amongst algae, and a few Modiolus modiolus present. Large clearings of sediment with Chone spp. and occasional Branchiomma bombyx.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Near horizontal plain of very soft mud with small quantity of shell fragments. Surface slightly uneven with rare burrows. Extensive clumps of Sabella pavonina with abundant Ascidiella aspersa and Antedon bifida on the Sabella pavonina. Mud surface festooned with small worm tubes (3-4mm) above surface with isolated individuals of; Thyone fusus, Amphitrite sp., Myxicola infundibulum, Mya truncata, Virgularia mirabilis, Sabella pavonina, clumps of Ascidiella aspersa with frequent Amphilectus fucorum and Halichondria bowerbanki, which in turn supported Leucosolinia sp.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Pinnacle in mouth of Loch Craignish. (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Steep/vertical tide-swept lower circalittoral, -19 to -34 m. Swiftia pallida frequent. Hydroids and erect bryozoans conspicuous.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Pinnacle in mouth of Loch Craignish. (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Top of bedrock ridge, from -14 to -17 m, with sparse Laminaria hyperborea and understorey of foliose red algae.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Pinnacle in mouth of Loch Craignish. (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Bryozoa has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Plain of boulders on muddy sand, sloping gradually from -34 to beyond -35 m. Pecten maximus and Ophiura albida on the sediment, Swiftia pallida frequent on the boulders, along with large numbers of bryozoans.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: Pinnacle in mouth of Loch Craignish. (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Rhodophycota has been removed from the species list for this record as more specific related taxa were also present, these are now marked as characterising. Steep bedrock with some silt cover, in upper circalittoral, -17 to -19 m. Foliose red algae common.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealocs survey
location_name: W of Liath Sgeir Bheag (Loch Craignish)
sample_comment: Lower circalittoral of steep bedrock from -24.5 m followed by boulders on bedrock, a fairly level plain of boulders with muddy sand between at -41.5 m. Fauna typical of slight shelter, with frequent Swiftia pallida, Terebratulina retusa and Iophionopsis nigrescens, and occasional Axinella infundibuliformis. Neocrania anomala also frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Large boulders and bedrock outcrops on muddy sediments shallower than 22 m BCD. Characterised by a wide variety of animal species but with Swiftia pallida, Diazonia violacea frequent.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: 30 degree mud slope, 21 -23 m BCD. Burrows frequent, Swiftia pallida attached to pebbles.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of coul Eilean (Outer Loch Melfort)
sample_comment: Gentle mud slope with Pennatula phosphorea, Virgularia mirabilis and Cerianthus lloydii.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
sample_comment: Soft sediment with a 30 degree slope with 3% shell gravel. Burrows and tracks frequent. Sediment meets bedrock at 11 m. Colonised by Virgularia mirabilis with frequent Liocarcinus puber and Pagurus bernhardus. Virgularia mirabilis most dense at 20 m.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E side of Loch Caolas (Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Plain of wave-marked coarse shell gravel, characterised by Neopentadactyla mixta and Ensis spp., with large numbers of Pomatoschistus pictus. Foliose algae attached to shells or drift in furrows. Eggs of Natica spp. present.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E side of Loch aColas (Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Level muddy gravel sediment with shells dominated by Brongniatella bryssoides and characterised by Megalomma [Branchiomma] vesiculosum, Gibbula magus and terebellid spp.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: E side of Loch Colas (Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Plain of firm muddy fine sand covered in a diatom film. The sediment contained many tubes and burrows and Turritella shells. The characterising species included Virgularia mirabilis (small) and Amphiura spp. This appeared a rich sediment community.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: SW of Eilean na Bruachain (Loch Caolisport)
sample_comment: Plain of mud at 15 m bcd with many empty Turritella communis shells. Abundant Amphiura filiformis with occasional small Virgularia mirabilis and occasional Ceriathus lloydii.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
location_name: N of Eilean an Ruisg (Loch Feochan)
sample_comment: A plain of coarse sediment (muddy gravel) with cobbles and pebbles and a few small boulders and shells. Dense stands of Laminaria saccharina with Chorda filum and filamentous browns and foliose reds growing profusely in clearings. Sabella pavonina common amongst algae, and a few Modiolus modiolus present. Large clearings of sediment with Chone spp. and occasional Branchiomma bombyx.

survey_name: 1989 UMBSM south Argyll and west Kintyre sealochs survey
